issuesFound.title: Missing page title
issuesFound.explanation: The <title> element is empty. Screen reader users rely on the page title to understand the site and page context immediately upon loading.
issuesFound.fix: Line 7: <title>Luminary Studio — Accessible Design & Productivity Tools</title>

issuesFound.title: Toggle button missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle button lacks an aria-expanded attribute. Screen reader users will not know if the menu is currently open or closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 41: <button type="button" class="nav-toggle" aria-expanded="false" aria-label="Toggle navigation menu">

issuesFound.title: Broken ARIA reference
issuesFound.explanation: The button references aria-controls="solutions-submenu", but the target ID "solutions-submenu" is on a <ul> that is marked as hidden. While the ID exists, the button itself lacks the aria-expanded state to manage it.

issuesFound.title: Landmark missing distinguishing label
issuesFound.explanation: There are multiple <nav> elements on this page (main navigation and account navigation), but this instance lacks an aria-label to distinguish it.

issuesFound.title: Non-descriptive link text
issuesFound.explanation: The link text "Read more" is non-descriptive when taken out of context. Screen reader users navigating by links will not know what they are reading more about.
issuesFound.fix: Line 126: Read more about Luminary Studio features

issuesFound.title: Image missing alt attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The hero dashboard image is missing an alt attribute. Even if considered decorative, it must have alt="" to be ignored by assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: Line 141:

issuesFound.title: Heading level skip
issuesFound.explanation: The heading structure jumps from <h2> (Line 237) to <h5> (Line 241) within the "How it works" section, skipping <h3> and <h4>.

issuesFound.title: Table header missing scope
issuesFound.explanation: The <th> elements "Feature", "Starter", "Pro", and "Enterprise" in the <thead> lack a scope="col" attribute in a table that also uses row headers.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 465: <th scope="col">Feature</th>
       Line 466: <th scope="col">Starter</th>
       Line 467: <th scope="col">Pro</th>
       Line 468: <th scope="col">Enterprise</th>

issuesFound.title: Form control missing accessible label
issuesFound.explanation: The "Email" input in the contact form has no associated <label> element, aria-label, or aria-labelledby.
issuesFound.fix: Line 725: Line 726:

issuesFound.title: Missing autocomplete attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The email input collects personal data but uses "work email", which is a valid compound value, but the field lacks a standard autocomplete token for the primary "email" purpose.
issuesFound.fix: Line 726: <input type="email" id="contact-email" name="email" autocomplete="email" required aria-required="true">

issuesFound.title: Missing legend
issuesFound.explanation: The <fieldset> containing interest checkboxes is missing a <legend> to provide a group label for the options.

issuesFound.title: Missing accessible name on link
issuesFound.explanation: The GitHub link contains an image with aria-hidden="true" and no visible text or aria-label on the link itself, leaving it with no accessible name.
issuesFound.fix: Line 865: <a href="https://github.com/luminary-studio" rel="noopener noreferrer" target="_blank" aria-label="Luminary Studio on GitHub">

issuesFound.title: focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible rule sets outline to none. While it sets an outline-offset, without a non-zero outline width, box-shadow, or border, no visible indicator is rendered.

issuesFound.title: focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus:not(:focus-visible)
issuesFound.explanation: This rule explicitly removes the outline for browsers or interactions not matching :focus-visible without providing any visual alternative, potentially blocking keyboard users on older browsers.

issuesFound.title: focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The button component removes the outline on focus-visible. Although it has a transition for box-shadow, no specific box-shadow or outline is defined in this rule-block to ensure visibility.

issuesFound.title: focus indicator removed without replacement on .header-inner > a:first-child:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The logo link removes the focus outline without providing a visible alternative like a border or box-shadow.

issuesFound.title: focus indicator removed without replacement on .nav-toggle:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle removes the focus outline without providing a visible alternative.

issuesFound.title: focus indicator removed without replacement on [id$="-submenu"] li a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown menu links remove the focus outline. While an outline-offset is provided, the lack of an outline width or box-shadow results in no visible indicator.

issuesFound.title: focus indicator removed without replacement on #search-input:focus
issuesFound.explanation: The search input removes the outline on focus. While it adds a box-shadow, box-shadow is invisible in Forced Colors Mode (High Contrast Mode), leaving the element without an indicator.

issuesFound.title: focus indicator removed without replacement on #search-input:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The search input explicitly removes the outline on focus-visible without any replacement.

issuesFound.title: focus indicator removed without replacement on #site-search button[type="submit"]:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button removes the focus outline without providing a visible alternative.

issuesFound.title: focus indicator removed without replacement on nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Account navigation links remove the focus outline without providing a visible alternative.

issuesFound.title: focus indicator removed without replacement on .feature-card a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Feature card links remove the focus outline without providing a visible alternative.

issuesFound.title: focus indicator removed without replacement on .filter-tabs button:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Filter category buttons remove the focus outline without providing a visible alternative.

issuesFound.title: focus indicator removed without replacement on .pricing-toggle button:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Pricing toggle buttons remove the focus outline without providing a visible alternative.

issuesFound.title: focus indicator removed without replacement on .faq-item dt button:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: FAQ accordion buttons remove the focus outline without providing a visible alternative.

issuesFound.title: focus indicator removed without replacement on .form-group input:focus, .form-group select:focus, .form-group textarea:focus
issuesFound.explanation: Form inputs remove the outline on focus. The provided box-shadow is insufficient as it disappears in Forced Colors Mode.

issuesFound.title: focus indicator removed without replacement on .form-group input:focus-visible, .form-group select:focus-visible, .form-group textarea:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Form inputs explicitly remove the outline on focus-visible without any replacement.

issuesFound.title: touch target below minimum size: .btn-sm height 20px
issuesFound.explanation: The small button variant has a min-height of 20px, which is below the WCAG 2.2 minimum requirement of 24px for interactive targets.

issuesFound.title: touch target below minimum size: .btn-icon height/width 20px
issuesFound.explanation: Icon buttons are explicitly sized to 20px by 20px, which is below the absolute minimum requirement of 24px.

issuesFound.title: touch target may be below 44px recommended size: .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The base button has a min-height of 32px. While it has padding, the total height may not reach the 44px recommended for optimal pointer use.

issuesFound.title: touch target may be below 44px recommended size: .nav-toggle
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile menu toggle has a min-height and min-width of 28px, which is significantly below the 44px recommendation.

issuesFound.title: touch target may be below 44px recommended size: #nav-menu > li > a
issuesFound.explanation: Navigation links have a min-height of 28px, which is below the 44px recommendation for touch targets.

issuesFound.title: touch target may be below 44px recommended size: [id$="-submenu"] li a
issuesFound.explanation: Submenu links have a min-height of 28px, which is below the 44px recommendation.

issuesFound.title: touch target may be below 44px recommended size: #search-input
issuesFound.explanation: The search input height is set to 28px, which is below the 44px recommendation.

issuesFound.title: touch target may be below 44px recommended size: #site-search button[type="submit"]
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button is sized at 28px by 28px, which is below the 44px recommendation.

issuesFound.title: touch target may be below 44px recommended size: .filter-tabs button
issuesFound.explanation: Category filter buttons have a min-height of 28px, which is below the 44px recommendation.

issuesFound.title: touch target may be below 44px recommended size: .pricing-toggle button
issuesFound.explanation: Pricing toggle buttons have a min-height of 28px, which is below the 44px recommendation.

issuesFound.title: touch target may be below 44px recommended size: .product-body .btn-secondary
issuesFound.explanation: Product CTA buttons have a min-height of 28px, which is below the 44px recommendation.

issuesFound.title: visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses "width: auto", "height: auto", and "overflow: visible", which fails to visually hide the content. Furthermore, the .not-sr-only class (Line 401) and .sr-only-focusable (Line 389) do not correctly manage visibility for AT.
issuesFound.fix: Line 374:   width: 1px; Line 375:   height: 1px; Line 378:   overflow: hidden; Line 379:   clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0);

issuesFound.title: motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .skip-link
issuesFound.explanation: The skip link uses a transform transition for movement, but the @media (prefers-reduced-motion) block on line 436 is empty and does not disable it.

issuesFound.title: motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The button component uses a transform transition for movement, but no reduced-motion override is provided.

issuesFound.title: motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .hero-ctas .btn-primary
issuesFound.explanation: The hero primary button uses a transform transition for movement without a reduced-motion override.

issuesFound.title: motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .hero-image img
issuesFound.explanation: The hero image uses a complex perspective transform transition that can cause motion sickness; the reduced-motion block on line 984 is empty.
issuesFound.fix: Line 985:   .hero-image img { transition: none; transform: none; }

issuesFound.title: motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .feature-card a::after
issuesFound.explanation: The "Learn more" arrow uses a transform transition for movement, but the reduced-motion block on line 1080 is empty.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1081:   .feature-card a::after { transition: none; transform: none; }

issuesFound.title: negative word-spacing impairs readability: body
issuesFound.explanation: The body text has a negative word-spacing of -0.2em, which crowds text and makes it difficult for users with low vision or dyslexia to read.

issuesFound.title: negative letter-spacing impairs readability: body
issuesFound.explanation: The body text has a negative letter-spacing of -0.05em, which reduces legibility.

issuesFound.title: negative letter-spacing impairs readability: h1, h2, h3, h4, h5, h6
issuesFound.explanation: Headings use a negative letter-spacing variable (--letter-spacing-tight), which can impair readability.

issuesFound.title: negative letter-spacing impairs readability: .hero-content h1
issuesFound.explanation: The hero heading uses --letter-spacing-tighter (-0.05em), which significantly reduces legibility.

issuesFound.title: negative letter-spacing impairs readability: .stat-value
issuesFound.explanation: Stat numbers use --letter-spacing-tighter, making the figures harder to distinguish.

issuesFound.title: link underline removed — colour alone distinguishes links from surrounding text
issuesFound.explanation: The global 'a' selector removes underlines. In body text (like .prose or paragraphs), this makes links indistinguishable from text for colorblind users.

issuesFound.title: focus indicator removed without replacement on #search-input:focus
issuesFound.explanation: The search input removes the outline on focus. While it adds a box-shadow, box-shadow is invisible in Forced Colors Mode (High Contrast Mode), leaving the element without an indicator.

issuesFound.title: focus indicator removed without replacement on #search-input:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The search input explicitly removes the outline on focus-visible without any replacement.

issuesFound.title: focus indicator removed without replacement on #site-search button[type="submit"]:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button removes the focus outline without providing a visible alternative.

issuesFound.title: focus indicator removed without replacement on nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Account navigation links remove the focus outline without providing a visible alternative.

issuesFound.title: focus indicator removed without replacement on .feature-card a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Feature card links remove the focus outline without providing a visible alternative.

issuesFound.title: focus indicator removed without replacement on .filter-tabs button:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Filter category buttons remove the focus outline without providing a visible alternative.

issuesFound.title: focus indicator removed without replacement on .pricing-toggle button:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Pricing toggle buttons remove the focus outline without providing a visible alternative.

issuesFound.title: focus indicator removed without replacement on .faq-item dt button:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: FAQ accordion buttons remove the focus outline without providing a visible alternative.

issuesFound.title: focus indicator removed without replacement on .form-group input:focus, .form-group select:focus, .form-group textarea:focus
issuesFound.explanation: Form inputs remove the outline on focus. The provided box-shadow is insufficient as it disappears in Forced Colors Mode.

issuesFound.title: focus indicator removed without replacement on .form-group input:focus-visible, .form-group select:focus-visible, .form-group textarea:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Form inputs explicitly remove the outline on focus-visible without any replacement.

issuesFound.title: touch target below minimum size: .btn-sm height 20px
issuesFound.explanation: The small button variant has a min-height of 20px, which is below the WCAG 2.2 minimum requirement of 24px for interactive targets.

issuesFound.title: touch target below minimum size: .btn-icon height/width 20px
issuesFound.explanation: Icon buttons are explicitly sized to 20px by 20px, which is below the absolute minimum requirement of 24px.

issuesFound.title: touch target may be below 44px recommended size: .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The base button has a min-height of 32px. While it has padding, the total height may not reach the 44px recommended for optimal pointer use.

issuesFound.title: touch target may be below 44px recommended size: .nav-toggle
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile menu toggle has a min-height and min-width of 28px, which is significantly below the 44px recommendation.

issuesFound.title: touch target may be below 44px recommended size: #nav-menu > li > a
issuesFound.explanation: Navigation links have a min-height of 28px, which is below the 44px recommendation for touch targets.

issuesFound.title: touch target may be below 44px recommended size: [id$="-submenu"] li a
issuesFound.explanation: Submenu links have a min-height of 28px, which is below the 44px recommendation.

issuesFound.title: touch target may be below 44px recommended size: #search-input
issuesFound.explanation: The search input height is set to 28px, which is below the 44px recommendation.

issuesFound.title: touch target may be below 44px recommended size: #site-search button[type="submit"]
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button is sized at 28px by 28px, which is below the 44px recommendation.

issuesFound.title: touch target may be below 44px recommended size: .filter-tabs button
issuesFound.explanation: Category filter buttons have a min-height of 28px, which is below the 44px recommendation.

issuesFound.title: touch target may be below 44px recommended size: .pricing-toggle button
issuesFound.explanation: Pricing toggle buttons have a min-height of 28px, which is below the 44px recommendation.

issuesFound.title: touch target may be below 44px recommended size: .product-body .btn-secondary
issuesFound.explanation: Product CTA buttons have a min-height of 28px, which is below the 44px recommendation.

issuesFound.title: visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses "width: auto", "height: auto", and "overflow: visible", which fails to visually hide the content. Furthermore, the .not-sr-only class (Line 401) and .sr-only-focusable (Line 389) do not correctly manage visibility for AT.
issuesFound.fix: Line 374:   width: 1px; Line 375:   height: 1px; Line 378:   overflow: hidden; Line 379:   clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0);

issuesFound.title: motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .skip-link
issuesFound.explanation: The skip link uses a transform transition for movement, but the @media (prefers-reduced-motion) block on line 436 is empty and does not disable it.

issuesFound.title: motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The button component uses a transform transition for movement, but no reduced-motion override is provided.

issuesFound.title: motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .hero-ctas .btn-primary
issuesFound.explanation: The hero primary button uses a transform transition for movement without a reduced-motion override.

issuesFound.title: motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .hero-image img
issuesFound.explanation: The hero image uses a complex perspective transform transition that can cause motion sickness; the reduced-motion block on line 984 is empty.
issuesFound.fix: Line 985:   .hero-image img { transition: none; transform: none; }

issuesFound.title: motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .feature-card a::after
issuesFound.explanation: The "Learn more" arrow uses a transform transition for movement, but the reduced-motion block on line 1080 is empty.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1081:   .feature-card a::after { transition: none; transform: none; }

issuesFound.title: negative word-spacing impairs readability: body
issuesFound.explanation: The body text has a negative word-spacing of -0.2em, which crowds text and makes it difficult for users with low vision or dyslexia to read.

issuesFound.title: negative letter-spacing impairs readability: body
issuesFound.explanation: The body text has a negative letter-spacing of -0.05em, which reduces legibility.

issuesFound.title: negative letter-spacing impairs readability: h1, h2, h3, h4, h5, h6
issuesFound.explanation: Headings use a negative letter-spacing variable (--letter-spacing-tight), which can impair readability.

issuesFound.title: negative letter-spacing impairs readability: .hero-content h1
issuesFound.explanation: The hero heading uses --letter-spacing-tighter (-0.05em), which significantly reduces legibility.

issuesFound.title: negative letter-spacing impairs readability: .stat-value
issuesFound.explanation: Stat numbers use --letter-spacing-tighter, making the figures harder to distinguish.

issuesFound.title: link underline removed — colour alone distinguishes links from surrounding text
issuesFound.explanation: The global 'a' selector removes underlines. In body text (like .prose or paragraphs), this makes links indistinguishable from text for colorblind users.

issuesFound.title: toggle function does not update aria-expanded on trigger element
issuesFound.explanation: The `open` and `close` functions in the `mobileNav` module toggle the visibility of the navigation drawer but never update the `aria-expanded` attribute on the trigger button. Screen reader users will not be informed that the menu has opened or closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1185: `_open = true; setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');` Line 1204: `_open = false; setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');`

issuesFound.title: toggle button does not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The `setPeriod` function in the `pricing` module updates the billing period but does not update the `aria-pressed` state of the period buttons. This makes it impossible for screen reader users to determine which billing cycle is currently active.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2065: `_periodBtns.forEach(btn => { const isActive = btn.getAttribute('data-period') === period; setAttr(btn, ARIA.PRESSED, String(isActive)); toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive); });`

issuesFound.title: form validation error not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The `_renderResult` function in the `inlineValidator` module adds an error class and message but does not set `aria-invalid="true"` on the input element. Screen readers will not announce the field as being in an error state.

issuesFound.title: aria-invalid not cleared on valid input
issuesFound.explanation: When a field is validated as correct, the `_renderResult` function removes error classes but does not set `aria-invalid="false"` or remove the attribute. The field may continue to be announced as invalid by screen readers.

issuesFound.title: aria-expanded not initialised on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.init` function does not set an initial `aria-expanded="false"` state on the hamburger trigger. Screen reader users will not know the button controls an expandable region until after the first interaction.

issuesFound.title: user action result not announced via aria-live region
issuesFound.explanation: The `_onViewToggle` handler changes the product grid layout between list and grid modes, but this change is not announced to screen readers. Users who cannot see the screen will be unaware that the presentation of data has changed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1965: `_productFilterState.viewMode = mode; liveRegion.announce('Switched to ' + mode + ' view');`

issuesFound.title: dynamically-hidden element not updated in accessibility tree
issuesFound.explanation: The `scrollToTop` module removes the `hidden` attribute from the button but does not manage `aria-hidden`. If the button is hidden via CSS opacity or transform, it may remain in the accessibility tree while invisible.

issuesFound.title: toggle trigger missing aria-controls
issuesFound.explanation: The filter tab buttons in the `productFilter` module control the visibility of items within the product grid, but they do not have `aria-controls` pointing to the grid's ID.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1935: `const gridId = srUtils.ensureId(_gridEl, 'product-grid'); qsa('[data-filter-value]', _tabsEl).forEach(btn => setAttr(btn, ARIA.CONTROLS, gridId));`

issuesFound.title: toggle function does not update aria-expanded on trigger element
issuesFound.explanation: The `open` and `close` functions in the `mobileNav` module toggle CSS classes and visibility but never update the `aria-expanded` attribute on the hamburger trigger button. Screen reader users will not be informed that the navigation menu has been deployed or retracted.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1185: `_open = true; setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');`; Line 1204: `_open = false; setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');`

issuesFound.title: toggle button does not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The `_onTabClick` handler in the `productFilter` module updates the active category but does not update the `aria-pressed` state of the filter buttons. This prevents screen reader users from identifying which filter is currently active in the set.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2115: `_productFilterState.activeCategory = value; qsa('[data-filter-value]', _tabsEl).forEach(btn => setAttr(btn, ARIA.PRESSED, btn.getAttribute('data-filter-value') === value));`

issuesFound.title: form validation error not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The `_renderResult` function in the `inlineValidator` module adds an error class and shows an error message but never sets the `aria-invalid` attribute on the input field. Screen readers will not announce the field as being in an error state when focus enters.

issuesFound.title: aria-invalid not cleared on valid input
issuesFound.explanation: When a field is corrected and becomes valid, the `_renderResult` function removes the error message but does not set `aria-invalid="false"` or remove the attribute. The field may continue to be announced as invalid by some screen readers.

issuesFound.title: aria-expanded not initialised on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.init` function does not set an initial `aria-expanded="false"` state on the navigation trigger. Assistive technologies may not announce the button as a toggle until the first interaction occurs.

issuesFound.title: user action result not announced via aria-live region
issuesFound.explanation: The `_onViewToggle` handler changes the product grid layout between 'grid' and 'list' modes, but this significant visual change is not announced to screen reader users.

issuesFound.title: dynamically-hidden element not updated in accessibility tree
issuesFound.explanation: The `_updateEmptyState` function in the `productFilter` module uses the `hidden` attribute to hide the empty state element but does not explicitly set `aria-hidden="true"`. Some older browser/screen reader combinations may still perceive the element.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav toggle function does not update aria-expanded on trigger element
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.toggle() function changes the visibility of the navigation drawer but does not update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button. Screen reader users will not be informed whether the navigation menu is open or closed, breaking WCAG 4.1.2 (Name, Role, Value).
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1443: function toggle() { _open ? close() : open(); }
Line 1443: function toggle() { _open ? close() : open(); setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, !_open); }

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance function does not update aria-expanded on trigger element
Product filter changes not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: When product filters are applied, the updated product count and visible cards change without announcement. Screen reader users are not informed of the dynamic content change.
issuesFound.fix: After applying filters, call liveRegion.announce with a message like "Showing X products".

issuesFound.title: Pricing period change not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: Switching billing periods (month/year) updates prices without announcing the change to screen readers. Users who rely on assistive technology may not know the prices have changed.
issuesFound.fix: After updating prices, call liveRegion.announce with a message like "Switched to annual billing, save 20%".

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open/close not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: Expanding or collapsing FAQ items does not announce the state change to screen readers. Users cannot hear whether the answer is now visible or hidden.
issuesFound.fix: In the open and close functions, call liveRegion.announce with the question title and state (e.g., "Answer expanded for [question]").

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation open/close not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: Opening or closing the mobile navigation menu does not announce the state change. Screen reader users cannot hear when the menu becomes available or is dismissed.
issuesFound.fix: In the open and close functions, call liveRegion.announce with "Navigation menu opened" or "Navigation menu closed".

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open/close not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: Opening or closing dropdown menus does not announce the state change. Screen reader users are not informed when a submenu appears or disappears.
issuesFound.fix: In _openInstance and _closeInstance, call liveRegion.announce with the menu label and state.

issuesFound.title: Form validation errors not setting aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: When a form field is invalid, the code adds error classes but does not set aria-invalid="true". Screen readers cannot programmatically detect the invalid state.
issuesFound.fix: In _renderResult, when result.valid is false, add setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true'). When valid, set to 'false'.

issuesFound.title: Scroll-to-top action not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: Clicking the scroll-to-top button scrolls the page without announcing the action. Screen reader users may not know the page has scrolled to the top.
issuesFound.fix: In _onClick, after scrolling, call liveRegion.announce("Scrolled to top of page").

issuesFound.title: Product filter changes not announced (JS-G duplicate)
issuesFound.explanation: User action (applying filters) changes visible content without live region announcement. Screen reader users are not informed of the result.
issuesFound.fix: After applying filters, call liveRegion.announce with a message like "Filter applied, showing X products".

issuesFound.title: Pricing period change not announced (JS-G duplicate)
issuesFound.explanation: User action (changing billing period) updates visible prices without live region announcement. Screen reader users may miss the price change.
issuesFound.fix: After updating prices, call liveRegion.announce with a message like "Billing period changed to annual".

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation open/close not announced (JS-G duplicate)
issuesFound.explanation: User action (toggling mobile nav) changes visible content without live region announcement. Screen reader users are not informed of the menu state.
issuesFound.fix: In open and close functions, call liveRegion.announce with "Navigation menu opened" or "Navigation menu closed".

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open/close not announced (JS-G duplicate)
issuesFound.explanation: User action (opening/closing dropdown) changes visible content without live region announcement. Screen reader users are not informed of the submenu state.
issuesFound.fix: In _openInstance and _closeInstance, call liveRegion.announce with the menu label and state.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open/close not announced (JS-G duplicate)
issuesFound.explanation: User action (expanding/collapsing FAQ) changes visible content without live region announcement. Screen reader users are not informed of the answer visibility.
issuesFound.fix: In open and close functions, call liveRegion.announce with the question title and state.

fpTitles: Product filter changes not announced (JS-G duplicate)
Dropdown menu open/close not announced (JS-G duplicate)
FAQ accordion open/close not announced (JS-G duplicate)

issuesFound.title: mobileNav open/close functions do not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav module's open() and close() functions toggle the visibility of the navigation drawer but do not update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button. Screen reader users will not be informed of the menu's expanded state.
issuesFound.fix: In the mobileNav module's open() function, add: setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true'); In the close() function, add: setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu open/close functions do not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu module's _openInstance() and _closeInstance() functions toggle dropdown panels but do not update aria-expanded on the trigger buttons. Screen reader users will not know whether dropdowns are expanded.
issuesFound.fix: In _openInstance(), add: setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true'); In _closeInstance(), add: setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: pricing toggle does not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing toggle (role="switch") cycles between monthly and annual billing but does not update aria-pressed. Screen reader users will not know the current toggle state.
issuesFound.fix: In the pricing module's _syncToggleState() function, add: setAttr(_toggleEl, ARIA.PRESSED, period === 'year' ? 'true' : 'false');

issuesFound.title: productFilter results not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: When filters are applied, the product count and visibility change are not announced via an aria-live region. Screen reader users may not know that content has updated.
issuesFound.fix: In productFilter.applyFilters(), after updating the count, add: liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.filterApplied?.(totalMatches) || `${totalMatches} products found`);

issuesFound.title: inlineValidator does not set aria-invalid on invalid fields
issuesFound.explanation: The inlineValidator marks fields invalid by adding a CSS class and error message but does not set aria-invalid="true". Screen reader users may not be alerted to the error state.
issuesFound.fix: In inlineValidator._renderResult(), when result.valid is false, add: setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'true');

issuesFound.title: inlineValidator does not clear aria-invalid on valid fields
issuesFound.explanation: When a field becomes valid, the inlineValidator removes error styling but does not clear the aria-invalid attribute (or set it to false). Screen readers may continue to announce the field as invalid.
issuesFound.fix: In inlineValidator._renderResult(), when result.valid is true, add: setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'false');

issuesFound.title: mobileNav aria-expanded not initialised on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation drawer is closed on page load, but the trigger button does not have aria-expanded="false" initialised. Screen reader users may not know the initial state.
issuesFound.fix: In mobileNav.init(), after obtaining _trigger, add: setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu aria-expanded not initialised on page load
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown panels are hidden on page load, but trigger buttons do not have aria-expanded="false" initialised. Screen reader users may not know the initial collapsed state.
issuesFound.fix: In dropdownMenu.init(), after registering each instance, add: setAttr(trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: mobileNav toggle trigger missing aria-controls
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile nav trigger button does not have an aria-controls attribute pointing to the drawer element. Screen reader users may not understand which element is controlled.
issuesFound.fix: In mobileNav.init(), after obtaining _trigger and _drawer, add: setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.CONTROLS, _drawer.id);

issuesFound.title: pricing billing period change not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: When the billing period is switched (monthly/annual), no live region announcement is made. Screen reader users may not be aware of the price updates.
issuesFound.fix: In pricing.setPeriod(), after updating the period, add: liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.pricingAnnual || 'Prices updated to annual billing');

issuesFound.title: mobileNav open/close not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: Opening and closing the mobile navigation drawer does not trigger a live region announcement. Screen reader users may not know when the menu state changes.
issuesFound.fix: In mobileNav.open(), add: liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navOpen || 'Navigation menu opened'); In mobileNav.close(), add: liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navClosed || 'Navigation menu closed');

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu open/close not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: Opening and closing dropdown menus does not trigger a live region announcement. Screen reader users may not know when dropdowns expand or collapse.
issuesFound.fix: In _openInstance(), add: liveRegion.announce('Dropdown opened'); In _closeInstance(), add: liveRegion.announce('Dropdown closed');

issuesFound.title: scrollToTop action not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: Clicking the scroll-to-top button scrolls the page but does not announce the action. Screen reader users may not be aware of the page position change.
issuesFound.fix: In scrollToTop._onClick(), after scrolling, add: liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.scrolledToTop || 'Scrolled to top of page');

issuesFound.title: mobileNav does not trap focus when drawer is open
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer opens, focus is not trapped inside it. Keyboard users may tab out of the drawer into the background, causing loss of context.
issuesFound.fix: In mobileNav.open(), after opening, create and activate a focus trap: _trap = createFocusTrap(_drawer); _trap.activate(); In close(), deactivate: if (_trap) _trap.deactivate();

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu does not trap focus when open
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown menu opens, focus is not trapped inside it. Keyboard users may tab out of the dropdown, breaking the interaction model.
issuesFound.fix: In _openInstance(), after opening, create and activate a focus trap: inst.trap = createFocusTrap(inst.panel); inst.trap.activate(); In _closeInstance(), deactivate: if (inst.trap) inst.trap.deactivate();

fpTitles: dropdownMenu does not trap focus when open

issuesFound.explanation: `.btn` includes a transition that animates `transform`, which creates movement. No corresponding `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)` rule disables this animation, violating users who prefer reduced motion.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers‑reduced‑motion override on .hero-ctas .btn-primary
issuesFound.explanation: `.hero-ctas .btn-primary` transitions `transform` (used for hover lift) without a reduced‑motion override, causing unwanted motion for users who request reduced motion.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers‑reduced‑motion override on .hero-ctas .btn-primary:hover

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers‑reduced‑motion override on .pricing-card--featured:hover
issuesFound.explanation: The featured pricing card’s hover state animates `transform: translateY(-4px) scale(1.01);` without a reduced‑motion override, causing motion for users who prefer reduced motion.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle does not update aria‑expanded on open
issuesFound.explanation: mobileNav.open adds CSS classes to show the drawer and locks scrolling, but never sets aria‑expanded on the hamburger button that controls the drawer. Screen‑reader users cannot know that the navigation has been expanded.
issuesFound.fix:
Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` inside mobileNav.open (and the corresponding `'false'` in mobileNav.close).  

---

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle does not update aria‑expanded on close
issuesFound.explanation: mobileNav.close removes the drawer’s CSS classes but does not reset aria‑expanded on the trigger button, leaving the button’s state inaccurate for assistive technology.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open does not set aria‑expanded on its trigger
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown is opened the panel’s hidden attribute is removed and a CSS class is added, yet the trigger button never receives an aria‑expanded update. Users of assistive technology cannot tell whether the menu is open.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu close does not clear aria‑expanded on its trigger
issuesFound.explanation: Closing a dropdown only removes CSS classes and adds hidden to the panel; the trigger’s aria‑expanded remains true, causing a stale state for screen readers.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open does not set aria‑expanded on the question button
issuesFound.explanation: Opening an FAQ item animates the panel and updates internal state, but the [data‑faq‑trigger] button never receives an aria‑expanded attribute change. Assistive technology cannot detect the expanded/collapsed state.
issuesFound.fix:
Insert `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` when the item is opened.  

---

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion close does not clear aria‑expanded on the question button
issuesFound.explanation: Closing an FAQ item hides the panel but leaves aria‑expanded on the trigger set to true, leaving the button’s state inaccurate.

issuesFound.title: Site‑search suggestion panel updates are not announced via a live region
issuesFound.explanation: When the search input gains focus the suggestion list is shown and populated, but no aria‑live region is updated to announce that suggestions are available. Keyboard‑only users receive no feedback.
issuesFound.fix:
After `suggestionListRenderer.show();` call `liveRegion.announce('Search suggestions available');` or update an existing live‑region element.  

---

issuesFound.title: Site‑search result count changes are not announced via a live region
issuesFound.explanation: The function updates a status element’s textContent but the element lacks role="status"/ aria‑live attributes, so changes to the number of suggestions are not spoken by screen readers.
issuesFound.fix:
Add `setAttr(_statusEl, 'role', 'status');` and `setAttr(_statusEl, 'aria-live', 'polite');` or use `liveRegion.announce(...)` when the count changes.  

---

issuesFound.title: Inline validator does not set aria‑invalid on fields that fail validation
issuesFound.explanation: When a field is invalid the code calls srUtils.markInvalid, which only adds aria‑describedby but never sets aria‑invalid to true. Assistive technologies therefore cannot detect the error state.
issuesFound.fix:
Replace `srUtils.markInvalid(field, errorEl);` with `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');` (and remove it on success).  

---

issuesFound.title: Newsletter form submission does not clear aria‑invalid on fields after a successful submit
issuesFound.explanation: After a successful subscription the form’s fields may still carry aria‑invalid from a previous error because the validator never removes it on success. This leaves the form in an erroneous state for screen readers.
issuesFound.fix:
After a successful submit, iterate over the form’s inputs and call `field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid');`.  

---

issuesFound.title: Product‑filter applyFilters updates the result count badge without a live‑region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When filters change, the visible count badge is updated, but no live‑region is used to announce the new number of matching products, leaving users of assistive technology unaware of the change.
issuesFound.fix:
Add a hidden element with role="status" and aria‑live="polite", and update its textContent (e.g., `liveRegion.announce(`${count} products found`);`).  

---

issuesFound.title: Pricing period toggle button does not set aria‑expanded or aria‑pressed appropriately for the associated region
issuesFound.explanation: The billing‑period toggle updates visual classes and ARIA‑pressed on the buttons, but the button that controls the price display region never receives aria‑expanded or aria‑controls, so screen readers cannot associate the toggle with the region it changes.
issuesFound.fix:
Ensure the toggle button has `setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-controls', panelId);` and update `aria-expanded` to reflect the open/closed state of the price panel.  

---

issuesFound.title: Testimonials carousel navigation (next/prev) changes slide content without a live‑region announcement in some cases
issuesFound.explanation: The next function moves to the next slide and calls liveRegion.announce, but if the slide change is triggered by the “play/pause” button the announcement is omitted, leaving users unaware of the slide change.
issuesFound.fix:
Ensure every path that changes the active slide (including the play/pause button) calls `liveRegion.announce` with the new slide number.  

---

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion “Open all” button does not announce that all items have been expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The openAll function expands every FAQ item but does not provide any audible feedback via a live region, so screen‑reader users cannot know that the operation completed.
issuesFound.fix:
After expanding all items, call `liveRegion.announce('All FAQ items expanded');`.  

---

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu focus management does not move focus into the panel when opened, violating keyboard‑navigation expectations
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown is opened via keyboard (Enter/Space), the code opens the panel but only moves focus to the first focusable child after a requestAnimationFrame. If the panel contains no focusable elements, focus remains on the trigger, which can trap keyboard users.
issuesFound.fix:
After opening, ensure focus is set to the panel itself (e.g., `inst.panel.setAttribute('tabindex', '-1'); inst.panel.focus();`).  

---  

These issue blocks each reference a specific line (or function) from the supplied JavaScript file and describe the accessibility problem, its severity, and an exact code‑level fix.

Testimonials carousel navigation (next/prev) changes slide content without a live‑region announcement in some cases

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation trigger does not update `aria-expanded` on toggle
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.toggle()` function calls `setExpanded()` which updates `aria-expanded` on the trigger, but the `open()` and `close()` helpers also call `setExpanded()` — however the initial state is never set, and the `setExpanded` call is missing when the drawer is closed via outside‑click or Escape key. This can leave the button’s ARIA state out of sync with the drawer.
issuesFound.fix:
Ensure every code path that opens or closes the drawer calls `setExpanded(_trigger, true/false, _drawer)`.  

---

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu triggers lack `aria-expanded` and do not set it on state change (JS‑A)
issuesFound.explanation: In `dropdownMenu._openInstance()` and `_closeInstance()` the trigger button’s visual state is toggled, but no `aria-expanded` attribute is set to reflect the open/closed state. Screen readers cannot know whether the dropdown is expanded.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion trigger missing `aria-expanded="true"` when opened (JS‑C)
issuesFound.explanation: In `faqAccordion.open(item)` the trigger button is given `aria-expanded="false"` on close, but when the item is opened the attribute is never set to `"true"`. Users of assistive technology cannot tell which FAQ is expanded.
issuesFound.fix:
Add `setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');` at the start of the `open` function.  

---

issuesFound.title: Pricing period buttons lack `aria-pressed` and never update it (JS‑B)
issuesFound.explanation: The period selector buttons (`[data-period]`) are used as a two‑state toggle group, but they never receive an `aria-pressed` attribute, nor is it updated when the active period changes. This violates the requirement for toggle buttons to expose their pressed state.

issuesFound.title: Pricing “annual/monthly” toggle switch missing `aria-pressed` (JS‑B)
issuesFound.explanation: The simple on/off switch (`[data-billing-toggle]`) toggles between monthly and annual billing, but it never sets `aria-pressed` to indicate its current state.
issuesFound.fix:
In `_onToggleClick()` after changing the period, call `setAttr(_toggleEl, ARIA.PRESSED, String(_pricingState.period === 'year'));` and initialise it to `false` on load.  

---

issuesFound.title: Pricing period change not announced to screen readers (JS‑C)
issuesFound.explanation: When the billing period changes, the UI updates price numbers but no live‑region announcement is made, so users are unaware of the change.
issuesFound.fix:
After `setPeriod()` finishes, add:  
```javascript
liveRegion.announce(
  `Billing period switched to ${BILLING_PERIODS[period].label.toLowerCase()}`
);
```  

---

issuesFound.title: Product filter count badge updates are not announced (JS‑C)
issuesFound.explanation: The product count badge (`[data-product-count]`) is updated with the number of visible products, but no ARIA live region is used, leaving screen‑reader users uninformed of the result of a filter.
issuesFound.fix:
Create a hidden live region (e.g., `
`) and, after `_updateCountBadge(count)`, set its text: ```javascript document.getElementById('filter-status').textContent = `${count} product${count !== 1 ? 's' : ''} found`; ``` ---

issuesFound.title: Search clear button is not keyboard‑operable (HTML‑A)
issuesFound.explanation: The clear button (`.search-clear`) is a `<button>` but it is hidden via CSS when the input is empty and shown only when the input has a value. When hidden, it is removed from the tab order, but the script does not restore focus to the input after clearing, potentially leaving keyboard users stranded.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion panels are not given `role="region"` when they are `<div>` (HTML‑A)
issuesFound.explanation: For FAQ items where the answer panel is a `<div>` (not a `<details>`), the script adds `role="region"` only if missing, but does not set an explicit `aria-label` or `aria-labelledby` when the panel lacks a heading. This can make the region’s purpose ambiguous.
issuesFound.fix:
Ensure each panel has `aria-labelledby` pointing to its trigger (already done) and also give it a descriptive `aria-label` if needed.  

---

issuesFound.title: Dynamic elements hidden via CSS classes are not updated with `aria-hidden` (JS‑F)
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer, dropdown menus, or FAQ panels are hidden by adding a CSS class (`is-hidden`, `dropdown--open` removed, etc.), the script does not set `aria-hidden="true"` on the hidden element. This leaves the element in the accessibility tree while invisible.
issuesFound.fix:
In each hide routine, after adding the hidden class, also call `setAttr(element, ARIA.HIDDEN, 'true');` and on show, `removeAttr(element, ARIA.HIDDEN);`.  

---

issuesFound.title: Toggle widgets lack `aria-controls` linking trigger to controlled region (JS‑E)
issuesFound.explanation: The trigger elements for the mobile navigation drawer, dropdown menus, and FAQ items have `aria-expanded` but no `aria-controls` attribute that points to the ID of the element they control. This breaks the relationship for assistive technologies.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period buttons do not have `aria-controls` (JS‑E)
issuesFound.explanation: The period selector buttons control the price display panels but lack `aria-controls` linking them to the region they affect.
issuesFound.fix:
When initializing the period buttons, assign each button an `aria-controls` that references the container holding the price values (e.g., the `.pricing-card` element).  

---

issuesFound.title: Live region updates for product filter “no results” state are missing (JS‑C)
issuesFound.explanation: When the product grid becomes empty, the UI shows an empty‑state element, but no live‑region announcement is made, leaving screen‑reader users unaware that no products match the filter.
issuesFound.fix:
After showing the empty‑state, announce:  
```javascript
liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.productNoResults || 'No products match your filters.');
```  

These issues cover all mandatory JavaScript ARIA sweeps (JS‑A through JS‑G) and the relevant HTML accessibility checks identified in the source code.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The :focus-visible selector sets outline: none but only provides outline-offset: 3px. An offset alone does not create a visible ring when the outline width is zero, making keyboard focus invisible to users.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus:not(:focus-visible)
issuesFound.explanation: This rule removes the outline for mouse users but provides no alternative visual indicator. If focus state is triggered by other means, the element will appear unfocused.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement in forced-colors media query
issuesFound.explanation: Inside @media (forced-colors: active), :focus-visible has outline: none with only an offset. In High Contrast Mode, box-shadows are ignored, and zero-width outlines are invisible, hiding focus completely.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The button focus state removes the outline and relies solely on outline-offset. Without a non-zero outline width, border, or box-shadow in this block, the focus ring is invisible.

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static and clip: auto, which makes the content fully visible instead of hiding it visually. This exposes screen-reader-only text to all users.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class uses transition: transform which causes spatial movement. There is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) rule to disable this animation for users who request reduced motion.
issuesFound.fix: Line 393: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .btn { transition: none; } .btn:active { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .hero-image img

issuesFound.fix: Line 773: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-image img { transform: none; transition: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The #site-search label.sr-only rule uses display: none, which hides the label from screen readers as well as visual users. Input labels must be available to AT.
issuesFound.fix: Line 583: #site-search label.sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body rule sets word-spacing: -0.2em, which compresses words together and reduces readability for users with cognitive disabilities.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .section-header p
issuesFound.explanation: The paragraph text uses #aaaaaa on a #ffffff background. The contrast ratio is approximately 1.86:1, which fails the 4.5:1 minimum for normal text.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .feature-card p
issuesFound.explanation: The card body text uses #aaaaaa on a #ffffff background. The contrast ratio is approximately 1.86:1, failing WCAG 1.4.3 Contrast.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .step-content p
issuesFound.explanation: The step content text uses #aaaaaa on a #ffffff background. The contrast ratio is approximately 1.86:1, failing WCAG 1.4.3 Contrast.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .product-body > p:not(.product-tagline)
issuesFound.explanation: The product description text uses #aaaaaa on a #ffffff background. The contrast ratio is approximately 1.86:1, failing WCAG 1.4.3 Contrast.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .input-hint
issuesFound.explanation: Input hint text uses #aaaaaa on a #f8fafc background. The contrast ratio is below 4.5:1, failing WCAG 1.4.3 Contrast.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .btn:active
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:active rule uses transform: translateY(1px) but is not disabled in the @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block. This may cause discomfort for users sensitive to motion.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .hero-ctas .btn-primary:hover
issuesFound.explanation: The hero button hover state uses transform: translateY(-1px) without a reduced-motion override. Users who prefer reduced motion will still see this movement.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .hero-image img
issuesFound.explanation: The hero image uses a 3D transform (rotateY/rotateX) which is not disabled in the prefers-reduced-motion block (lines 663-665 are empty).

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative on a
issuesFound.explanation: The global rule removes text-decoration from all links. Without a global alternative (like bold font or border), links are distinguished by color alone, failing WCAG 1.4.1 Use of Color.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative on footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a
issuesFound.explanation: Footer links remove underlines and rely on color change for hover. In the rest state, they are indistinguishable from surrounding text without color perception.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1339: footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a { text-decoration: underline; }

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative on .social-links a
issuesFound.explanation: Social links remove underlines and rely on color change for hover. In the rest state, they are indistinguishable from surrounding text without color perception.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav toggle function does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav open() and close() functions toggle the CSS class and hidden attribute on the drawer but fail to update the aria-expanded state on the trigger button. Screen reader users will not know if the menu is open or closed.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1406: setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');
  Line 1426: setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger missing initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav init() function finds the trigger but does not set the initial aria-expanded="false" attribute. The trigger's state is undefined on page load for assistive technology.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger missing aria-controls
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav init() function identifies the trigger and drawer but does not associate them using aria-controls. Screen readers cannot programmatically determine which element the button controls.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1465: const drawerId = srUtils.ensureId(_drawer, 'mobile-nav'); setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.CONTROLS, drawerId);

issuesFound.title: mobileNav overlay visibility not synced with aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav open() and close() functions toggle the hidden attribute on the overlay element but do not update aria-hidden. The overlay remains in the accessibility tree when visually hidden.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav toggle action not announced
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav toggle() function changes the navigation state but does not announce the change to screen readers via a live region. Users are not informed that the menu has opened or closed.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle functions do not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu _openInstance() and _closeInstance() functions show/hide the panel but do not update aria-expanded on the trigger button. The button state remains static despite the panel visibility changing.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1599: setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');
  Line 1612: setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu trigger missing initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu register() function sets aria-haspopup but does not set the initial aria-expanded="false" on the trigger. The initial state is not communicated to assistive technology.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu panel visibility not synced with aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu _openInstance() and _closeInstance() functions toggle the hidden attribute on the panel but do not update aria-hidden. The panel remains perceivable to screen readers when visually hidden.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle action not announced
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu _onTriggerClick() function toggles the menu state but does not announce the change to screen readers. Users are not informed when a dropdown opens or closes.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open function does not set aria-expanded to true
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion open() function removes the hidden attribute from the panel but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger. The close() function correctly sets it to false, creating an inconsistent state.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion panel visibility not synced with aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion open() and close() functions toggle the hidden attribute on the panel but do not update aria-hidden. The panel content remains in the accessibility tree when collapsed.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion toggle action not announced
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion toggle() function changes the expansion state but does not announce the change to screen readers. Users are not informed which question was expanded or collapsed.

issuesFound.title: pricing period change not announced
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing setPeriod() function updates the displayed prices but does not announce the billing period change to screen readers. Users are not informed that the prices have switched to monthly or annual.

issuesFound.title: productFilter count update not announced
issuesFound.explanation: The productFilter _updateCountBadge() function updates the text content of the count badge but does not announce the change to screen readers. Users are not informed how many products match the filter.

issuesFound.title: productFilter tab change not announced
issuesFound.explanation: The productFilter _onTabClick() function applies a new filter but does not announce the change to screen readers. Users are not informed that the product list has been filtered.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch input missing initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The siteSearch suggestionListRenderer.mount() function sets up the combobox attributes but does not set the initial aria-expanded="false" on the input. The initial state of the suggestion list is not communicated.

issuesFound.title: scrollToTop action not announced
issuesFound.explanation: The scrollToTop _onClick() function scrolls the page to the top but does not announce the action to screen readers. Users are not informed that the page position has changed.

issuesFound.title: scrollToTop button visibility not synced with aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The scrollToTop _update() function toggles the hidden attribute on the button but does not update aria-hidden. The button remains in the accessibility tree when visually hidden.

issuesFound.title: stickyHeader hidden state not synced with aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The stickyHeader _onScroll() function toggles the CSS class to hide the header but does not update aria-hidden. The header remains in the accessibility tree when visually hidden.

issuesFound.title: srUtils markInvalid does not set aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The srUtils markInvalid() function links the error message via aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the field. Screen readers do not know the field is in an error state.

issuesFound.title: srUtils markValid does not remove aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The srUtils markValid() function removes the error description but fails to remove or set aria-invalid="false" on the field. The field may remain marked as invalid in the accessibility tree.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav toggle function does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav module's open() and close() functions toggle the visibility of the navigation drawer but fail to update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button (_trigger). Screen reader users are not informed whether the menu is currently open or closed.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1675: setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');
  Line 1695: setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle function does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu module's _openInstance() and _closeInstance() functions show and hide the dropdown panel but do not update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button (inst.trigger). This prevents assistive technology from conveying the current state of the dropdown.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1833: setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');
  Line 1850: setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: siteSearch combobox does not update aria-expanded on input
issuesFound.explanation: The siteSearch module's _open() and _close() functions manage the visibility of the suggestion listbox but do not update the aria-expanded attribute on the search input (_inputEl). As a combobox, the input must reflect whether the popup is expanded.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2383: setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');
  Line 2395: setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open function does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion module's open() function expands the answer panel but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button (item.trigger). The close() function correctly sets it to "false", creating an inconsistent state where screen readers are not informed when an item opens.

issuesFound.title: pricing toggle does not update aria-pressed or aria-checked state
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing module's _syncToggleState() function updates visual classes on period buttons and the toggle track but does not update aria-pressed on buttons or aria-checked on the switch element (_toggleEl). The state change is not conveyed to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2934: setAttr(btn, ARIA.PRESSED, String(isActive));
  Line 2941: setAttr(_toggleEl, ARIA.CHECKED, String(isYear));

issuesFound.title: srUtils validation helpers do not manage aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The srUtils.markInvalid() and srUtils.markValid() functions manage error descriptions but fail to set or clear the aria-invalid attribute on the field. Screen readers rely on aria-invalid to announce that a field has an error.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav state change not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav module's open() and close() functions emit custom events but do not announce the state change (e.g., "Navigation menu opened") to screen readers via a live region. Users are not aware the menu state has changed.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu state change not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu module's _openInstance() and _closeInstance() functions emit events but do not announce the dropdown state change to screen readers. Users are not informed when a menu expands or collapses.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1834: liveRegion.announce('Menu expanded');
  Line 1851: liveRegion.announce('Menu collapsed');

issuesFound.title: productFilter result count change not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The productFilter module's applyFilters() function updates the product count badge and grid but does not announce the new result count (e.g., "Showing 12 products") to screen readers. Users are unaware of the filter impact.

issuesFound.title: pricing period change not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing module's setPeriod() function updates prices and toggle state but does not announce the billing period change (e.g., "Prices switched to annual billing") to screen readers. Users are not informed of the context change.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion state change not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion module's open() and close() functions emit events but do not announce the answer expansion or collapse to screen readers. Users are not informed when content is revealed or hidden.

issuesFound.title: scrollToTop action not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The scrollToTop module's _onClick() function scrolls the page to the top but does not announce this action to screen readers. Users may lose context of their new position on the page.

issuesFound.title: cookieConsent banner state not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The cookieConsent module's _showBanner() and _hideBanner() functions display and remove the consent dialog but do not announce its presence or dismissal to screen readers. Users may not know the banner has appeared.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 4320: liveRegion.announce('Cookie consent banner displayed');
  Line 4350: liveRegion.announce('Cookie consent banner closed');

issuesFound.title: Icon-only button missing accessible name (ThemeToggle)
issuesFound.explanation: The ThemeToggle component renders an IconButton without passing the required aria-label prop. The LABELS object is defined but never used. Screen reader users will hear "button" without knowing its function (switching themes).

issuesFound.title: Icon-only button missing accessible name (MobileNav close)
issuesFound.explanation: The IconButton used to close the mobile navigation panel does not have an aria-label prop. Screen reader users will not understand that this button closes the menu.

issuesFound.title: Landmark region missing accessible name (HeroSection)
issuesFound.explanation: The HeroSection uses a <section> element without aria-label or aria-labelledby. This landmark cannot be distinguished by screen reader users navigating by region.

issuesFound.title: Landmark region missing accessible name (StatsBar)
issuesFound.explanation: The StatsBar section contains an h2 with id="stats-heading", but the <section> element does not reference it via aria-labelledby. The landmark is unnamed.

issuesFound.title: Landmark region missing accessible name (SkipLinks)
issuesFound.explanation: The SkipLinks component renders a <nav> element without an aria-label. If there are other navs on the page (which there are), this landmark is indistinguishable.

issuesFound.title: Landmark region missing accessible name (DesktopNav)
issuesFound.explanation: The DesktopNav component renders a <nav id="main-nav"> without an aria-label. It is indistinguishable from other navigation landmarks on the page.

issuesFound.title: Card article missing accessible name (PlanCard)
issuesFound.explanation: The PlanCard article contains a heading with id={headingId}, but the <article> element does not use aria-labelledby to reference it. Screen reader users navigating by article will not hear the plan name.

issuesFound.title: Loading spinner missing aria-hidden (Button)
issuesFound.explanation: When isLoading is true, the Button component renders a spinner span without aria-hidden="true". This decorative icon may be announced redundantly alongside the VisuallyHidden loading text.

issuesFound.title: Required field marker missing aria-hidden (FormGroup)
issuesFound.explanation: The visual asterisk marker for required fields is rendered in a span without aria-hidden="true". Screen readers may announce "asterisk" unnecessarily since the word "required" is already provided visually hidden.

issuesFound.title: Decorative clone list missing aria-hidden (IntegrationsBar)
issuesFound.explanation: The second list in IntegrationsBar is a visual clone for looping animation. It lacks aria-hidden="true", causing screen readers to read the integration list twice.

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .btn

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .hero-ctas .btn-primary

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .hero-image img

issuesFound.fix: Line 715-718: `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-image img { transform: none; transition: none; } .hero-image img:hover { transform: none; } }`

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .step-number

issuesFound.title: mobileNav toggle functions missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation drawer toggle functions open() and close() add and remove CSS classes to show or hide the drawer, but they never update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button to reflect the current state. Screen readers will not announce whether the menu is expanded or collapsed.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle functions missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown menu controller opens and closes panels by setting the open property and toggling CSS classes, but it does not set aria-expanded="true" or aria-expanded="false" on the trigger button to communicate the panel's visibility state to assistive technologies.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion toggle functions missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The FAQ accordion open() and close() functions animate the panel height and manage the hidden attribute, but they do not update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button. This prevents screen readers from announcing whether an answer is expanded or collapsed.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch combobox missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The site search input implements a combobox pattern but the _open and _close functions do not toggle aria-expanded on the input element to indicate whether the suggestion listbox is displayed or hidden.

issuesFound.title: pricing period buttons missing aria-pressed updates
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period toggle buttons (monthly/quarterly/year) act as a toggle group where one button is always pressed, but the setPeriod function only toggles CSS classes and does not update aria-pressed to indicate the selected state to screen readers.

issuesFound.title: productFilter view mode buttons missing aria-pressed updates
issuesFound.explanation: The grid/list view toggle buttons cycle between two states but do not update the aria-pressed attribute to indicate which view mode is currently active.

issuesFound.title: testimonialsCarousel play button missing aria-pressed updates
issuesFound.explanation: The play/pause toggle button controls auto-rotation but does not update aria-pressed to reflect whether auto-play is currently active or paused.

issuesFound.title: inlineValidator not setting aria-invalid on validation
issuesFound.explanation: When a field fails validation, the _renderResult function adds error classes and updates the error message element, but it never sets aria-invalid="true" on the invalid field or aria-invalid="false" when the field becomes valid. Screen readers rely on this attribute to convey validation state.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger missing aria-controls attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger button does not have an aria-controls attribute pointing to the drawer element it controls. This prevents screen readers from understanding the relationship between the button and the drawer.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav aria-expanded not initialized at page load
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger button does not have aria-expanded="false" set when the page loads and the drawer is closed. This violates the requirement that ARIA states must reflect the initial state on page load.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu aria-expanded not initialized at page load
issuesFound.explanation: When dropdown triggers are registered, the code ensures the panel is hidden but does not initialize aria-expanded="false" on the trigger button to reflect that the panel is initially closed.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion aria-expanded not initialized at page load
issuesFound.explanation: While the FAQ accordion checks for existing aria-expanded attributes, it does not explicitly set aria-expanded="false" on triggers that lack the attribute when the accordion initializes with all panels closed.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch aria-expanded not initialized at page load
issuesFound.explanation: The search input acts as a combobox but does not initialize with aria-expanded="false" to indicate that the suggestion list is initially closed.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav drawer missing aria-hidden when closed
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer is closed, it is hidden via CSS class removal but remains in the accessibility tree because aria-hidden="true" is not set. This can cause screen readers to access content that should be hidden.

issuesFound.title: pricing billing period change not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: When the user switches between monthly and annual billing, the prices update visually but the change is not announced to screen readers via a live region, leaving users unaware that the content has changed.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion state changes not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: When FAQ items are expanded or collapsed, the state change is not announced to screen readers, making it difficult for users to know whether their action succeeded unless they manually check the content.

issuesFound.title: scrollToTop action not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: When the scroll-to-top button is activated, the page scrolls to the top and focus moves to the skip link, but there is no announcement confirming the action to screen readers.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu state changes not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: When dropdown menus open or close, the state change is not announced to screen readers, which can leave users unaware that a menu has appeared or disappeared.
