issuesFound.title: mobileNav toggle functions do not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation drawer is toggled using CSS classes, but the trigger button's aria-expanded attribute is never updated to reflect the open/closed state. Screen readers cannot announce whether the navigation is expanded or collapsed.

issuesFound.title: Form validation does not set aria-invalid on invalid fields
issuesFound.explanation: When form validation fails, the markInvalid function adds an error message via aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the field. Screen readers cannot identify which fields contain errors.

issuesFound.title: Form validation does not clear aria-invalid on valid fields
issuesFound.explanation: When form validation passes, the markValid function removes the error description but does not set aria-invalid="false" or remove the aria-invalid attribute, leaving stale invalid state on the field.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period buttons do not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period toggle buttons (monthly/annual) indicate the active state via CSS classes only. They lack aria-pressed attributes to communicate the pressed state to screen readers.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu triggers missing aria-expanded initialisation
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown trigger buttons are initialised with aria-haspopup and aria-controls, but aria-expanded="false" is not set initially, violating the requirement that ARIA states reflect the initial closed state on page load.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch input missing aria-expanded initialisation
issuesFound.explanation: The search input acts as a combobox with an expandable suggestion list, but aria-expanded is not initialised to "false" when the list is closed on page load.

issuesFound.title: productFilter tab changes not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: When users click filter tabs to change product categories, the visible content updates but no aria-live announcement is made. Screen reader users are not informed that filtering has occurred or how many results are showing.

issuesFound.title: pricing period changes not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: When the billing period is toggled between monthly and annual, the price values update visually but the change is not announced to screen readers via a live region.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav open/close not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: Opening and closing the mobile navigation drawer changes the visible UI significantly, but no announcement is made to inform screen reader users that the navigation state has changed.

issuesFound.title: activeFilterChips removal not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: When users remove a filter chip, the product grid updates but the removal action and the resulting filter state are not announced to screen readers.

issuesFound.title: Contact form inputs missing autocomplete attributes
issuesFound.explanation: The contact form fields (first name, last name, email, etc.) are personal data inputs that lack autocomplete attributes. The JavaScript does not set autocomplete on these fields, preventing browsers from assisting users with autofill.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav drawer missing aria-hidden when closed
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer is closed, it is hidden via CSS but remains in the accessibility tree because aria-hidden is not set to "true". Screen readers can still access the hidden content.

issuesFound.title: Pricing toggle element should be native button
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period toggle element (data-billing-toggle) may be implemented as a div or span. The JavaScript adds role="switch" and tabindex="0" but it should be a native <button> element for proper keyboard accessibility and semantics.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle functions missing aria-expanded updates

issuesFound.title: siteSearch combobox missing aria-expanded updates

issuesFound.title: productFilter tab buttons missing aria-pressed

issuesFound.title: pricing period buttons missing aria-selected

issuesFound.fix: Line 3299: Add `setAttr(btn, ARIA.SELECTED, String(isActive));` immediately after `toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive);`

issuesFound.title: pricing toggle switch missing aria-checked

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger missing aria-controls

issuesFound.fix: Line 1372: Add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.CONTROLS, srUtils.ensureId(_drawer, 'nav-drawer'));` immediately after the null checks for _trigger and _drawer.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu aria-expanded not initialized
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown trigger buttons do not have aria-expanded initialized to "false" on page load when the dropdown panels are hidden by default.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch aria-expanded not initialized

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid missing aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The utility function that marks form fields as invalid links the error message via aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the field, so screen readers cannot identify the invalid state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1087: Add `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'true');` immediately after the addDescribedBy call.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid missing aria-invalid reset
issuesFound.explanation: The utility function that clears validation state removes the error description but does not set aria-invalid="false" or remove the aria-invalid attribute to indicate the field is now valid.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1106: Add `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'false');` or `removeAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID);` immediately after updating the aria-describedby attribute.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav drawer not removed from accessibility tree when hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation drawer is shown and hidden using CSS classes only, without setting aria-hidden="true" when closed. Although the drawer may be hidden with display:none via CSS, the JavaScript should explicitly manage aria-hidden to ensure the element is removed from the accessibility tree when not visible.

  Line 1325: Add `removeAttr(_drawer, ARIA.HIDDEN);` in the open() function after adding classes.

issuesFound.title: pricing period change not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: Changing the billing period updates the displayed prices, but the change in billing period and prices is not announced to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3290: Add `liveRegion.announce(period === 'year' ? STRINGS.announce.pricingAnnual : STRINGS.announce.pricingMonthly);` after emitting the pricing:periodchange event.

issuesFound.title: scrollToTop action not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: Clicking the scroll to top button scrolls the page to the top, but this navigation action is not announced to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1614: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.scrolledToTop || 'Scrolled back to top of page');` after the setTimeout that handles focus.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav focus trap not activated
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation drawer opens and traps focus in the DOM reference (`_trap` variable is declared but never assigned or activated), but the code never creates or activates the focus trap, allowing keyboard focus to escape the drawer when it is open.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1326: Add `_trap = createFocusTrap(_drawer, { returnFocus: _trigger });` after setting _open = true.
  Line 1327: Add `_trap.activate();` immediately after creating the trap.
  Line 1352: Change the existing line to `if (_trap) { _trap.deactivate(); _trap = null; }` to properly clean up the trap.

fpTitles: dropdownMenu aria-expanded not initialized
pricing period change not announced via live region

issuesFound.title: NavDropdown disclosure trigger missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown trigger button controls a sub-menu visibility managed by the isOpen state variable, but lacks aria-expanded to communicate the open/closed state to screen reader users. This prevents users from knowing whether the menu is currently expanded.

issuesFound.title: MobileNav submenu trigger missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The button toggles the visibility of the mobile navigation submenu sections using the isExpanded state, but does not expose this state via aria-expanded, leaving assistive technology users unaware of the current expansion state.

issuesFound.title: SiteHeader mobile toggle missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle button manages the isOpen state for the off-canvas menu but fails to communicate this state via aria-expanded, violating the disclosure pattern requirements.

issuesFound.title: FaqItemComponent accordion trigger missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The FAQ accordion button toggles the visibility of the answer panel using the isOpen state variable but omits aria-expanded, preventing screen reader users from determining whether the panel is currently expanded or collapsed.

issuesFound.title: StarRating widget missing role and decorative stars not hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The star rating container lacks role="img" to identify it as a graphic, and the individual star character spans are not hidden from assistive technology, causing redundant announcements after the parent aria-label is read.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~680: {stars.map((filled, i) => ({filled ? '★' : '☆'}))}

issuesFound.title: MobileNav close IconButton missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The icon-only close button inside the mobile navigation panel has no aria-label, leaving screen reader users without any indication of the button's purpose.

issuesFound.title: TestimonialsCarousel next button missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The next slide button contains only a decorative character and lacks an aria-label, preventing screen reader users from understanding its function.

issuesFound.title: TextInput component missing aria-invalid mapping
issuesFound.explanation: The component accepts an invalid prop to style the field as erroneous but fails to map this to the aria-invalid attribute, so screen reader users cannot programmatically determine that the field contains invalid data.

issuesFound.title: Textarea component missing aria-invalid mapping
issuesFound.explanation: The component accepts an invalid prop for visual error styling but does not expose this state to assistive technology via aria-invalid, preventing screen reader users from identifying the invalid field.

issuesFound.title: Select component missing aria-invalid mapping
issuesFound.explanation: The component accepts an invalid prop for styling but omits the aria-invalid attribute, leaving screen reader users unaware when the select field has a validation error.

issuesFound.title: NewsletterForm email input broken aria-describedby reference
issuesFound.explanation: The input references an error message ID 'newsletter-email-error' via aria-describedby when in error state, but no element with that ID exists in the document because FormGroup was not passed the errorId prop, breaking the connection between the field and its error message.

issuesFound.title: ContactForm name input broken aria-describedby reference
issuesFound.explanation: The input conditionally references 'contact-name-error' via aria-describedby, but FormGroup does not render an ErrorMessage with that ID because the errorId prop was not provided, resulting in a dangling reference to a non-existent element.

issuesFound.title: ContactForm email input broken aria-describedby reference
issuesFound.explanation: The input references 'contact-email-error' via aria-describedby when validation fails, but no element exists with this ID because FormGroup lacks the errorId prop, severing the programmatic link between the field and its error description.

issuesFound.title: ContactForm subject input broken aria-describedby reference
issuesFound.explanation: The input references 'contact-subject-error' via aria-describedby for error messages, but the corresponding ErrorMessage element is never rendered because FormGroup was not supplied with errorId and errorMessage props.

issuesFound.title: ContactForm message textarea broken aria-describedby error reference
issuesFound.explanation: The textarea conditionally references 'contact-message-error' in its aria-describedby, but this ID does not exist in the DOM because FormGroup does not receive the errorId prop, preventing screen readers from announcing the error message.

issuesFound.title: FilterTabs toggle buttons missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The filter buttons function as toggle buttons that persistently indicate the active filter state, but they lack aria-pressed to communicate whether they are currently selected to assistive technology users.

issuesFound.title: BillingToggle monthly button missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The monthly billing button acts as a toggle that remains pressed when selected, but it does not expose this state via aria-pressed, preventing screen reader users from understanding which billing period is active.

issuesFound.title: BillingToggle annual button missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The annual billing button functions as a persistent toggle indicating the selected billing period but lacks aria-pressed to convey its pressed state to assistive technology.

issuesFound.title: SkipLinks navigation landmark missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The page contains multiple nav elements, but this skip link navigation lacks an aria-label, making it impossible for screen reader users to distinguish it from other navigation landmarks.

issuesFound.title: DesktopNav navigation landmark missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The desktop navigation landmark appears alongside other nav elements on the page but has no aria-label or aria-labelledby, preventing screen reader users from identifying its specific purpose.

issuesFound.title: MobileNav body navigation landmark missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation panel contains a nav element that lacks an accessible name, making it indistinguishable from other navigation regions when using screen reader landmark navigation.

issuesFound.title: HeroSection landmark missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The hero section is a major page region but lacks aria-labelledby referencing its visible h1 heading, making it difficult for screen reader users to identify the section when navigating by landmarks.

issuesFound.title: StatsBar landmark missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The statistics bar is a distinct page section containing a heading with id="stats-heading", but the section lacks aria-labelledby to expose this heading as the landmark's accessible name.

issuesFound.title: PlanCard article missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing plan card is an article element that contains a heading with id={headingId}, but it lacks aria-labelledby to use that heading as its accessible name, making the card unidentifiable in article navigation.

issuesFound.title: Button component missing aria-busy during loading
issuesFound.explanation: When the isLoading prop is true, the button displays a loading spinner but does not communicate the busy state to assistive technology via aria-busy, preventing users from understanding that the button is currently processing.

issuesFound.title: DesktopNav current page link missing aria-current
issuesFound.explanation: The navigation link indicating the current page uses visual styling via the item.current flag but lacks aria-current="page", preventing screen reader users from programmatically identifying which page is current.

issuesFound.title: MobileNav current page link missing aria-current
issuesFound.explanation: The active navigation link in the mobile menu is visually distinguished by the item.current flag but does not expose this state via aria-current, making it impossible for screen reader users to identify the current page.

issuesFound.title: IntegrationsBar marquee clone missing aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The duplicated list of integration logos used for the marquee visual effect is not hidden from assistive technology, causing screen reader users to hear the entire list of integrations twice.

issuesFound.title: MobileNav backdrop interactive div missing button semantics
issuesFound.explanation: The backdrop element functions as a clickable control to close the navigation but is implemented as a div with onClick, lacking role="button", tabIndex, and keyboard event handlers, making it inaccessible to keyboard users.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~890: <div role="button" aria-label="Close navigation" tabIndex={0} onClick={onClose} onKeyDown={(e) => e.key === 'Enter' && onClose()} className="mobile-nav-backdrop" ... />

issuesFound.title: Modal overlay interactive div missing button semantics
issuesFound.explanation: The modal backdrop is clickable to close the dialog but uses a div with onClick without role="button", tabIndex, or keyboard support, preventing keyboard users from activating it.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1500: <div role="button" aria-label="Close dialog" tabIndex={0} onClick={onClose} onKeyDown={(e) => e.key === 'Enter' && onClose()} className="modal-overlay" ...>

issuesFound.title: Modal dialog missing aria-modal attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The aria-modal="true" attribute is incorrectly placed on the overlay div instead of the dialog div itself, failing to signal to assistive technology that the dialog is modal and that the content outside should be inert.

issuesFound.title: MobileNav section trigger missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The button toggles a sub-menu section but does not have aria-expanded to indicate the state of the controlled content. This prevents screen reader users from understanding whether the section is open or closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line: <button type="button" aria-controls={sectionId} aria-expanded={isExpanded} className="mobile-nav__link mobile-nav__link--trigger" onClick={() => setOpenSectionId(isExpanded ? null : item.href)}>

issuesFound.title: FaqItemComponent trigger button missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The accordion trigger button lacks aria-expanded to communicate whether the answer panel is currently expanded or collapsed. This is required for the disclosure pattern to be accessible.
issuesFound.fix: Line: <button type="button" id={btnId} aria-controls={panelId} aria-expanded={isOpen} className="faq-item__trigger" onClick={() => onToggle(item.id)}>

issuesFound.title: MobileNav close button missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The icon-only close button lacks an aria-label, so screen reader users cannot identify its purpose.
issuesFound.fix: Line: <IconButton aria-label="Close navigation menu" onClick={onClose} className="mobile-nav__close">

issuesFound.title: TestimonialsCarousel previous button missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The icon-only previous slide button lacks an aria-label, making it unidentifiable to screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line: <IconButton aria-label="Previous testimonial" aria-controls={trackId} onClick={handlePrev} className="testimonials-track__prev">

issuesFound.title: TestimonialsCarousel next button missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The icon-only next slide button lacks an aria-label, making it unidentifiable to screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line: <IconButton aria-label="Next testimonial" aria-controls={trackId} onClick={handleNext} className="testimonials-track__next">

issuesFound.title: BillingToggle annual button missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period toggle button visually indicates selection but lacks aria-pressed to communicate the pressed state to assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: Line: <button type="button" id="billing-annual" aria-pressed={value === 'annual'} className={['billing-toggle__btn', value === 'annual' ? 'billing-toggle__btn--active' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} onClick={() => onChange('annual')}>

issuesFound.title: FilterTabs buttons missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The filter toggle buttons visually indicate the active state but lack aria-pressed to communicate which filter is currently selected to screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line: <button type="button" aria-pressed={activeFilter === tab.value} className={['filter-tab-btn', activeFilter === tab.value ? 'filter-tab-btn--active' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} tabIndex={activeFilter === tab.value ? 0 : -1} onClick={() => onChange(tab.value)}>

issuesFound.title: DesktopNav landmark missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The navigation landmark lacks an aria-label. Since there are multiple nav elements on the page (including mobile nav and footer nav), screen reader users cannot distinguish between them.

issuesFound.title: MobileNav internal landmark missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The internal navigation landmark inside the mobile menu lacks an aria-label, making it indistinguishable from other navigation regions.

issuesFound.title: SkipLinks landmark missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The skip links navigation landmark lacks an aria-label to identify its purpose.

issuesFound.title: HeroSection landmark missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The section landmark lacks an aria-label or aria-labelledby. It should reference the visible heading "hero-heading" to provide a navigable landmark name.

issuesFound.title: StatsBar landmark missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The section landmark lacks an aria-label or aria-labelledby. It should reference the existing "stats-heading" to provide a navigable landmark name.

issuesFound.title: PlanCard article missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The article element in the pricing grid lacks aria-labelledby to associate it with its heading, making it difficult for screen reader users to identify the specific plan card when navigating by region.

issuesFound.title: DesktopNav current link missing aria-current
issuesFound.explanation: The current page link is visually indicated but lacks aria-current="page", so screen reader users cannot identify which page is currently active.
issuesFound.fix: Line: <a href={item.href} aria-current={item.current ? 'page' : undefined} className={['nav-link', item.current ? 'nav-link--current' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')}>

issuesFound.title: MobileNav current link missing aria-current
issuesFound.explanation: The current page link in the mobile navigation is visually indicated but lacks aria-current="page".
issuesFound.fix: Line: <a href={item.href} aria-current={item.current ? 'page' : undefined} className={['mobile-nav__link', item.current ? 'mobile-nav__link--current' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} onClick={onClose}>

issuesFound.title: Button component missing aria-busy when loading
issuesFound.explanation: When the button is in a loading state (isLoading=true), it lacks aria-busy to communicate to assistive technology that the element is being updated.
issuesFound.fix: Line: <button type={type} disabled={isDisabled} aria-disabled={isDisabled || undefined} aria-busy={isLoading} className={['btn', `btn--${variant}`, `btn--${size}`, className].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} {...rest}>

issuesFound.title: StarRating decorative stars not hidden from AT
issuesFound.explanation: The individual star characters are not hidden from assistive technology despite the container having an aria-label. Screen readers may announce the Unicode characters separately from the label.
issuesFound.fix: Line: <span key={i} className={filled ? 'star star--filled' : 'star star--empty'} aria-hidden="true">

issuesFound.title: IntegrationsBar clone list not hidden from AT
issuesFound.explanation: The duplicated list used for visual marquee looping is not hidden from assistive technology, causing duplicate content to be announced.

issuesFound.title: PlanCard CTA link missing descriptive accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The call-to-action link uses generic text ("Get started for free") without indicating which plan it refers to. In a grid of cards, screen reader users cannot distinguish which plan each button corresponds to.
issuesFound.fix: Line: <a href={plan.ctaHref} aria-label={`${plan.ctaLabel} — ${plan.name} plan`} className={['btn', plan.featured ? 'btn--primary' : 'btn--outline', 'btn--md'].filter(Boolean).join(' ')}>

issuesFound.title: TextInput component missing aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The TextInput component accepts an invalid prop to style the input as erroneous, but it does not map this to the aria-invalid attribute. Screen readers cannot perceive the error state programmatically, so users are unaware when field validation fails.

issuesFound.title: Textarea component missing aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The Textarea component accepts an invalid prop for styling but fails to set aria-invalid. Assistive technology users cannot detect when the textarea content is invalid.

issuesFound.title: Select component missing aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The Select component accepts an invalid prop for styling but does not expose aria-invalid. Screen readers cannot announce the error state for the dropdown.

issuesFound.title: Button component missing aria-busy during loading
issuesFound.explanation: When isLoading is true, the button shows a spinner and loading text but lacks aria-busy. Screen readers do not announce that the button is processing, leaving users uncertain if the action was triggered.

issuesFound.title: ThemeToggle IconButton missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The IconButton is invoked without an aria-label prop. The button contains only an emoji icon, so screen readers announce it as "button" with no context, preventing users from understanding its purpose.

issuesFound.title: MobileNav close IconButton missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The IconButton used to close the mobile navigation panel lacks an aria-label. It contains only the "✕" character, which may not be announced clearly, leaving users unaware of the button's function.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 481: <IconButton
    aria-label="Close navigation menu"
    onClick={onClose}
    className="mobile-nav__close"
  >

issuesFound.title: TestimonialsCarousel previous button missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The IconButton for the previous slide lacks an aria-label. It contains only the "‹" character, so screen readers cannot convey that it navigates to the previous testimonial.

issuesFound.title: TestimonialsCarousel next button missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The IconButton for the next slide lacks an aria-label. It contains only the "›" character, preventing screen reader users from understanding its purpose.

issuesFound.title: MobileNav disclosure button missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The button that expands and collapses submenu sections has aria-controls but no aria-expanded attribute. Screen readers cannot announce whether the submenu is currently open or closed.

issuesFound.title: FaqItemComponent disclosure button missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The FAQ trigger button toggles the answer panel but lacks aria-expanded. Users cannot perceive whether the answer is currently visible or hidden.

issuesFound.title: HeroSection video toggle missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The button toggles video playback state and visually changes icon, but lacks aria-pressed. Screen readers cannot communicate whether the video is currently playing or paused.

issuesFound.title: BillingToggle buttons missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period toggle buttons show visual selection state but lack aria-pressed. Screen readers cannot convey whether monthly or annual billing is selected.

issuesFound.title: DesktopNav current link missing aria-current
issuesFound.explanation: The link representing the current page has a visual "current" class but lacks aria-current="page". Screen readers cannot identify which page is active in the navigation.

issuesFound.title: MobileNav current link missing aria-current
issuesFound.explanation: The active page link in the mobile menu has a visual current indicator but no aria-current="page", preventing screen readers from announcing the current location.

issuesFound.title: SkipLinks navigation landmark missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The SkipLinks component renders a nav element without an aria-label. Since there are multiple nav elements on the page, screen readers cannot distinguish this skip navigation from others.

issuesFound.title: DesktopNav navigation landmark missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The desktop navigation nav element lacks an aria-label. With multiple nav elements present, users cannot distinguish the main navigation from others.

issuesFound.title: MobileNav internal navigation missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The nav element wrapping the mobile menu items lacks an aria-label, making it indistinguishable from other navigation landmarks when using screen reader landmark navigation.

issuesFound.title: HeroSection landmark missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The hero section element lacks aria-labelledby or aria-label. As a major page region, it should be labelled by its heading to support landmark navigation.

issuesFound.title: StatsBar landmark missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The statistics section lacks aria-labelledby despite having a visible heading. Screen reader users cannot identify this landmark when browsing by region.

issuesFound.title: PlanCard article missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing card article element lacks aria-labelledby. The component defines headingId but never applies it to the article, so screen readers cannot identify the card's name.

issuesFound.title: StarRating decorative stars not hidden from assistive technology
issuesFound.explanation: The individual star characters (★ and ☆) are not marked with aria-hidden. The container already has an aria-label describing the rating, so the individual stars create redundant noise for screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 247: <span key={i} className={filled ? 'star star--filled' : 'star star--empty'} aria-hidden="true">

issuesFound.title: FormGroup required marker announces bare asterisk
issuesFound.explanation: The visual asterisk marker for required fields is not hidden from assistive technology. Screen readers announce "star" or "asterisk" which is meaningless without context, especially since "(required)" is already provided via VisuallyHidden.

issuesFound.title: Section component labelledby prop not applied
issuesFound.explanation: The Section component accepts a labelledby prop but never applies it to the rendered element via aria-labelledby. When consumers pass this prop, the landmark remains unlabelled.

issuesFound.title: NewsletterForm email input aria-describedby references non-existent element
issuesFound.explanation: The email input sets aria-describedby="newsletter-email-error" when invalid, but FormGroup is not passed errorId="newsletter-email-error", so no element with that ID exists in the DOM. Screen readers cannot find the error message.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 757: <FormGroup
    label="Email address"
    htmlFor="newsletter-email"
    errorId="newsletter-email-error"
    error={email.state.touched ? email.state.error : undefined}
  >

issuesFound.title: ContactForm name input aria-describedby references non-existent element
issuesFound.explanation: The name input sets aria-describedby="contact-name-error" when invalid, but FormGroup lacks the errorId prop, so the error message is not linked via ID.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 817: <FormGroup
    label="Full name"
    htmlFor="contact-name"
    required
    errorId="contact-name-error"
    error={name.state.touched ? name.state.error : undefined}
  >

issuesFound.title: ContactForm email input aria-describedby references non-existent element
issuesFound.explanation: The email input sets aria-describedby="contact-email-error" when invalid, but FormGroup does not receive errorId="contact-email-error", breaking the association.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 833: <FormGroup
    label="Email address"
    htmlFor="contact-email"
    required
    errorId="contact-email-error"
    error={email.state.touched ? email.state.error : undefined}
  >

issuesFound.title: ContactForm subject input aria-describedby references non-existent element
issuesFound.explanation: The subject input sets aria-describedby="contact-subject-error" when invalid, but FormGroup is missing the errorId prop, so screen readers cannot locate the error text.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 849: <FormGroup
    label="Subject"
    htmlFor="contact-subject"
    required
    errorId="contact-subject-error"
    error={subject.state.touched ? subject.state.error : undefined}
  >

issuesFound.title: ContactForm message textarea aria-describedby references non-existent element
issuesFound.explanation: The message textarea sets aria-describedby including "contact-message-error" when invalid, but FormGroup does not receive errorId="contact-message-error", leaving the reference broken.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 865: <FormGroup
    label="Message"
    htmlFor="contact-message"
    required
    errorId="contact-message-error"
    hint={`Up to ${MSG_MAX} characters.`}
    error={message.state.touched ? message.state.error : undefined}
  >

issuesFound.title: IntegrationsBar cloned list not hidden from assistive technology
issuesFound.explanation: The duplicated list used for the marquee visual effect lacks aria-hidden. Screen readers encounter the same content twice, creating duplicate announcements of integration partners.

issuesFound.title: Modal backdrop click target not keyboard accessible
issuesFound.explanation: The modal backdrop div has an onClick handler to close the modal but lacks role="button", tabIndex={0}, and keyboard event handlers. Keyboard-only users cannot activate this click-to-close functionality.
